ops.text: PRE-REGISTRATION E30.7 (lean-vs-scaffolded judge-invocation equivalence — shared instrument characterization; NOT in the original plan, added to unblock Wave 0's cost breach). Question: does a LEAN claude CLI invocation (v6 judge prompt as system, no Claude Code tool/framework scaffolding, thinking off — via spawnllm ClaudeConfig) reproduce the SCAFFOLDED `claude -p` judge's verdicts closely enough that a prompt/method selected under lean transfers to the deployed judge? The deployed pipeline judge (cc_transcript.judge → spawnllm ClaudeCliBackend, sonnet/medium) carries the ~58K framework per call; lean strips it, cutting cost ~10x ($0.12→~$0.01/call). Method ($0 new frames; ~$8 cap, charged to E30 instrument budget not any experiment cap): score a FIXED shared 50-row dev subset (reuse E33's dev50_ids seed-33) under BOTH invocations, sonnet v6, each row once, temperature-default; persist per-row verdicts. Metrics, computed once: (1) lean-vs-scaffolded binary is_steering verdict agreement (raw + Cohen's κ); (2) auditor-agreement under each invocation (does lean track the gold as well as scaffolded?); (3) category agreement. Pre-registered ladder: raw verdict agreement ≥0.95 → CERTIFY lean for all Wave 0 prompt-scoring (E33/E34/E35 run fully lean, results transfer, caveat lifted); 0.90–0.95 → lean for SCREENING only, final winner+baseline scored SCAFFOLDED on test (raise the affected caps to absorb scaffolded test, within envelope); <0.90 → deployed judge materially depends on scaffolding → all scoring scaffolded → raise E33/E34/E35 caps to true cost and I reassess portfolio fit. VERIFY: independent recompute of the agreement/κ from the two persisted verdict JSONLs + confirm the lean call's input-token count is far below 58K (scaffolding actually stripped).
